user.title: Set up JinjaBoard
user.description: JinjaBoard renders Jinja2-templated Lovelace dashboards from files on disk. After setup, create a dashboard and set its YAML content to a `strategy` block pointing at your template file — see the README for an example. The frontend resource is registered automatically if Lovelace uses storage-mode resources; if it's YAML-mode, the next step will show what to add.

yaml_resources.title: Add the frontend resource manually
yaml_resources.description:
Lovelace is running in YAML resource mode, so JinjaBoard can't register its frontend module automatically. Add this to your `configuration.yaml` and restart Home Assistant:

```yaml
lovelace:
  resources:
    - url: {resource_url}
      type: module
```

init.title: Manage authorized template files
init.description: {count} file(s)/folder(s) currently authorized. A dashboard, view, or section `template` must be on this list to render — anything else is rejected.

add_entry.title: Authorize a file or folder
add_entry.description: Path is relative to the Home Assistant config directory, same as a dashboard's `strategy.template`. Enable "Folder" to authorize every file recursively under that directory instead of a single file.

remove_entry.title: Remove an authorized file or folder

error.path_traversal: That path escapes the Home Assistant config directory.

abort.no_entries: No files or folders are currently authorized.
